user_message: Can you extract the QBR preparation from this email thread for Alpine Ski House?

input_artifacts.content:
From: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-01 16:33 EST
Subject: Re: QBR preparation Discussion - Next Steps

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with QBR preparation. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
Date: 2024-02-10 15:25 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Alpine Ski House QBR preparation

Just checking in on the QBR preparation proposal. Our COO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-19 12:34 EST
Subject: Re: QBR preparation Discussion - Next Steps

There's been a change in our timeline for QBR preparation. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Alpine Ski House: the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk

handoffs.condition: MSX records need dark-period hygiene catchup

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF41-I trap (MSX hygiene catchup proposals don't acknowledge dark-period staleness); produces complete Context reconstruction, re-qualification assessment, response draft (appropriate tone), stakeholder
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF41-I failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF41-I failure mode (MSX hygiene catchup proposals don't acknowledge dark-period staleness)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct QBR preparation decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF41-I -- MSX hygiene catchup proposals don't acknowledge dark-period staleness
